extraction.one_line_summary: DEWULF Thomas won from 9-rider breakaway sprint after catching Garcia Cortina's 35km-to-go solo attack on local climb near Gijón
tactical_patterns: Garcia Cortina attacked 35km out on local climb but time-trial effort failed when chasers organized as group of four.
Contador attacked 10km from finish attempting to bridge to teammate Pozzovivo in break, revealing Tinkoff's willingness to sacrifice GC positioning.
Nine-rider breakaway regrouped on final climb despite Garcia Cortina's sustained solo effort, showing value of conservation over early aggression.

rider_signals.DEWULF Thomas: positive: Won sprint from regrouped break despite not being strongest on climbs, showing positioning discipline trumps raw power
rider_signals.GARCIA CORTINA Ivan: negative: Solo attack 35km out in home region failed as time-trial effort unsustainable against organized chase
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: neutral: Attacked 10km from finish to reach teammate Pozzovivo, indicating team tactics prioritized stage hunt over GC defense
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: positive: Moved into top-15 overall from breakaway effort, showing late-race GC opportunities from non-threatening break positions
